track_listing.track: 'Lightning Rose' (LP-Freedom At Point Zero) Aborted part way through as the record was too warped to play properly.

transcript.text: ...minutes of Led Zeppelin in concert.
Alright, let's have another number. Here's Led Zeppelin with their version of Willie Dixon's You Shook Me.
Listen carefully.
We gotta, we gotta put a close, a close to everything.
I'd like to introduce, as we go out, I'd like to introduce Led Zeppelin to you.
On bass guitar, John Paul Jones. John Paul Jones.
On drums, John Bonham.
Lead guitar, Jimmy Page.
Our most requested archive session,
Led Zeppelin has recorded on the 27th of the 6th of 1969.
And the tracks you heard were
How Many More Times, You Shook Me,
The Instrumental White Summer,
Dazed and Confused, Can't Cut Your Baby,
and Communication Breakdown.
Ten years ago, the producer was Jeff Griffin,
and the balance engineer was a gentleman in short trousers.
His name was Tony Wilson.
Just to run through some of the letters that have asked for that session,
we have one from Richard Turner of Darlington in County Durham,
one from Michael Robinson of, I think it's Gweesborough in Cleveland.
Anyway, Michael, you know where you live.
Michael Havelock from Loughborough University in Loughborough,
Mark Nicholson of Malvern College in Malvern,
Keith Evans of Merthyr Tydfil Mid Glamorgan in South Wales,
and R. Harrod of Lowestoft.
and also a very nice letter from a lady by the name of Catherine Moore
who lives in Hadfield, that's the one near Doncaster.
I hope all Led Zeppelin fans around the country...
That was nice, I never heard the original broadcast,
it was just pleasant and wonderful to sit here and listen to that.
Next week, ACDC.
Tune in to Radio 1 this Saturday at 6.30
and you'll hear a whole hour's worth of one of Britain's outstanding new wave bands,
The Jam.
Recorded live at the Rainbow, a performance not to be missed,
The Jam in concert at 6.30 this Saturday on Radio 1 and Stereo VHF.
It's 22 to midnight.
No Friday night connection tonight
because of the hold-up in the post over the Christmas period,
but it will return next week.
Here's Jefferson Starship.
But we can't have Tony Wilson with his finger on the record
trying to push it round all night long, can we, Mr Wilson?
But a very good effort.
We've already put a quid on the record
to see if we can get it going, 250p pieces.
It's known as a bowed record.
we apologize we'll try and sort it out in the interim period we'll continue the vigil
